# Implementation Plan: E-Commerce Shop Module

**Branch**: `001-shop-module` | **Date**: 2026-01-11 | **Spec**: [specs/001-shop-module/spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-shop-module/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Implement a lightweight e-commerce module using Next.js (App Router), Builder.io, and Stripe Hosted Checkout. The system will feature product listings (SSG from Builder.io), a client-side cart (Context + LocalStorage), and a secure checkout flow.
Technical approach: Hybrid validation strategy where frontend displays cached CMS data for performance, but backend API routes validate prices against authoritative Stripe IDs during session creation.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x, Node.js 18+ (Next.js 14 environment)
**Primary Dependencies**:

- Frontend: `react`, `@builder.io/react`, `stripe-js` (optional, for redirect)
- Backend: `stripe` (Node.js SDK), `next` (API Routes)
- UI: `tailwindcss`, `lucide-react` (icons)
  **Storage**:
- Client: `localStorage` (Cart state)
- CMS: Builder.io (Product content)
- Payment/Order: Stripe (hosted)
  **Testing**: `jest`, `@testing-library/react` (Component tests)
  **Target Platform**: Vercel (Next.js)
  **Project Type**: Monorepo Web Application (`apps/website`, `packages/shared-ui`)
  **Performance Goals**: < 2s TTI for product pages, < 500ms API response for checkout session creation.
  **Constraints**:
- Security: Stripe Secret Key MUST remain server-side.
- Data: Simple products only (no variants).
- Persistence: Cart survives tab close.

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

- [x] **2.1 Monorepo Modularity**: Logic placed in `apps/website` and reusable UI in `packages/shared-ui`.
- [x] **2.2 Platform Specialization**: Using Next.js/Builder.io for shop (marketing/content focused).
- [x] **2.3 Design Consistency**: Will use `packages/shared-ui` components where applicable.
- [x] **2.4 Type Safety**: All new code will be TypeScript.
- [x] **2.5 Infrastructure as Code**: Configuration changes (env vars types) will be committed.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-shop-module/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (OpenAPI/Types)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
apps/website/
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── shop/               # Shop Pages
│   │   │   ├── page.tsx        # Product Listing
│   │   │   └── [slug]/page.tsx # Product Detail
│   │   └── api/
│   │       ├── checkout/       # Checkout Session Endpoint
│   │       │   └── route.ts
│   │       └── webhooks/       # Stripe Webhook Handler
│   │           └── stripe/
│   │               └── route.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   └── shop/               # Shop-specific components (CartDrawer, ProductCard)
│   ├── context/
│   │   └── CartContext.tsx     # Global Cart State
│   └── lib/
│       └── stripe/             # Stripe server-side helpers
packages/shared-ui/
├── src/
│   └── components/             # Reusable UI (Buttons, Modals)
```

**Structure Decision**: Adheres to Next.js App Router conventions within the existing Monorepo structure.
